and **AFC U-17 Championship**, is a [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") competition, organised by the [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") (AFC) held once every two years for Asian under-17 teams that also serves as a qualification tournament for the [FIFA U-17 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-17_World_Cup "FIFA U-17 World Cup"). The top 8 countries qualify to participate in the FIFA U-17 World Cup. Up to 2000, the tournament was for U-16 teams. Between 2002 and 2006 it was held as an under-17 tournament. Between 2008 and 2020, it was switched back to a U-16 Championship. The AFC then returned it to an under-17 tournament starting from 2023.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Moreover, the tournament would also be rebranded from the "AFC U-16 Championship" to the "AFC U-17 Asian Cup".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

first_paragraph: The **AFC U-17 Asian Cup**, formerly known as the **AFC U-16 Championship** and **AFC U-17 Championship**, is a [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") competition, organised by the [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") (AFC) held once every two years for Asian under-17 teams that also serves as a qualification tournament for the [FIFA U-17 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-17_World_Cup "FIFA U-17 World Cup"). The top 8 countries qualify to participate in the FIFA U-17 World Cup. Up to 2000, the tournament was for U-16 teams. Between 2002 and 2006 it was held as an under-17 tournament. Between 2008 and 2020, it was switched back to a U-16 Championship. The AFC then returned it to an under-17 tournament starting from 2023.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Moreover, the tournament would also be rebranded from the "AFC U-16 Championship" to the "AFC U-17 Asian Cup".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

AFC U-17 Asian Cup

The **AFC U-17 Asian Cup**, formerly known as the **AFC U-16 Championship** and **AFC U-17 Championship**, is a [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") competition, organised by the [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") (AFC) held once every two years for Asian under-17 teams that also serves as a qualification tournament for the [FIFA U-17 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-17_World_Cup "FIFA U-17 World Cup"). The top 8 countries qualify to participate in the FIFA U-17 World Cup. Up to 2000, the tournament was for U-16 teams. Between 2002 and 2006 it was held as an under-17 tournament. Between 2008 and 2020, it was switched back to a U-16 Championship. The AFC then returned it to an under-17 tournament starting from 2023.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Moreover, the tournament would also be rebranded from the "AFC U-16 Championship" to the "AFC U-17 Asian Cup".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On 24 May 2024, AFC announced that the tournament will be held annually from 2026.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
